message:
docs(fp-io): correct default_schema() fallback claims (br-bn7x / fd90.57)

Self-review surfaced a doc-vs-code mismatch: two docstrings claimed
"None falls back to default_schema()" but no code path actually
implements that fallback. The default_schema() trait method exists
as a capability probe backends can override, but neither
table_exists_in_schema's default impl, the rusqlite override, nor
list_sql_tables's free-fn wrapper consult it.

Affected docstrings:
  - SqlConnection::table_exists_in_schema (fd90.17)
  - list_sql_tables (fd90.20)

Both now accurately describe pass-through behavior: schema=None
goes to the backend unchanged. Backends MAY consult
default_schema() in their own override logic, but the trait/wrapper
machinery does not apply it. No code change.

429 fp-io tests pass; clippy -D warnings clean; ubs Critical=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-bkl2","title":"fd90.37: SqlColumnSchema::autoincrement field for column-level autoincrement metadata","description":"Add SqlColumnSchema::autoincrement: bool field completing SQLAlchemy.Inspector.get_columns() parity. The dict shape includes 'autoincrement' alongside name/type/nullable/default/primary_key/comment. SQLite detection: a column qualifies as autoincrement when its declared_type is INTEGER (case-insensitive) AND primary_key_ordinal == Some(0) — that's the SQLite rowid-alias rule. Optional AUTOINCREMENT keyword doesn't change the property pandas cares about. Other backends: PG SERIAL/IDENTITY columns true; MySQL AUTO_INCREMENT modifier true. 7 existing SqlColumnSchema struct-literal sites bulk-updated with autoincrement: false. Tests cover INTEGER PRIMARY KEY auto-detected, INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT auto-detected, non-INTEGER PRIMARY KEY (TEXT PRIMARY KEY) NOT autoincrement, non-PK INTEGER NOT autoincrement, PG-like override returning explicit true.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T21:54:22.358151459Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T21:58:06.465257562Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T21:58:06.462698591Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd90","review","sql"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-bmz6","title":"fp-conformance: 4 unhandled FixtureOperation variants break compile","description":"Observed: cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib fails with 5x E0004 'non-exhaustive patterns' for FixtureOperation::DataFrameApplySemAxis0, DataFrameApplyProdAxis1, DataFrameApplyProductAxis1, DataFrameApplyNuniqueAxis0 (variants added at lines 336/347/352/377 of crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs).

Match-arm sites that need updating (line numbers from current HEAD):

  - 9712 (?)
  - 10367 (?)
  - 14509 (?)

Fix: add match arms following the same pattern as existing DataFrameApply* variants (DataFrameApplyMean, DataFrameApplySkew, etc). The 4 new variants likely correspond to the existing fp-frame DataFrame.apply('sem', axis=0), apply('prod', axis=1), apply('product', axis=1), apply('nunique', axis=0) gates that another agent (cod) is implementing in the broader ikkn bead family. Restore compile so other beads can land.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:42:07.676419104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:48:51.373528981Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:48:51.371665925Z","close_reason":"shipped 5c1450f: 4 new FixtureOperation variants (DataFrameApplySemAxis0, DataFrameApplyProdAxis1, DataFrameApplyProductAxis1, DataFrameApplyNuniqueAxis0) wired into 5 exhaustive match sites (CC-005 capability gate + 2 dataframe-aggregate dispatches + 2 operation-set arms), routing through frame.apply(name, axis) canonical path. fp-conformance: compile restored, 340/12 baseline. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-bn7x","title":"fd90.57: doc fix - default_schema() fallback claim is not implemented anywhere","description":"Two docstrings on table_exists_in_schema (fd90.17) and list_sql_tables (fd90.20) claim that when schema is None the impl falls back to default_schema(). No code actually does this fallback - schema=None just passes through to backend list_tables(None) / table_exists(table_name). Backends that DO want this behavior would have to implement it in their own list_tables/table_exists_in_schema overrides. Fix: remove the misleading 'fallback to default_schema()' phrase from both doc comments. Document instead that schema=None passes through to the backend unchanged, and that backends MAY consult default_schema() for their own fallback logic if desired. No code change.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:14:19.452141118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:16:33.398910971Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:16:33.396393470Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","review","sql"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-bovg","title":"Add series_rolling_sum/std/min/max edge case coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:27:27.905346719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:28:12.065585916Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:28:12.063822058Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-boyr","title":"[HIGH] conformance: 1249 golden fixtures have no pandas-version provenance — silent drift against pandas releases","description":"Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses skill's Fixture Provenance rule + the "fixtures can become stale" concern.

**Observation.** crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/ contains 1249 JSON fixture files. Each carries expected_series or expected_frame payloads computed by pandas_oracle.py at some generation point. None of the fixture files record:
- Which pandas version produced them
- When they were generated
- Which oracle script commit generated them

The fixtures/README.md describes the generation workflow but the Reference line says "pandas (system install, typically 2.x)" — no version pin. .github/workflows/ci.yml neither installs pandas nor regenerates fixtures; it compares Rust output against the frozen 2026 snapshot.

**Concrete risk.** Pandas 2.3.0, 3.0.0, etc. will land. When a pandas point release changes a semantic (they do — see https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/ changelog), our 1249 fixtures keep reflecting the OLD behavior. Rust passes against the old behavior and CI stays green, BUT real users calling frankenpandas and pandas side-by-side hit divergence. The longer the fixtures age without regeneration, the wider the drift.

**Severity.** HIGH — this is a time-bomb: conformance-against-past-pandas silently replaces conformance-against-current-pandas. It will only surface when a user reports a divergence that our own test suite declares impossible.

**Fix shape:**
1. Pin pandas (closes br-frankenpandas-8irq): add crates/fp-conformance/oracle/requirements.txt with pandas==X.Y.Z. CI installs exactly this version.
2. Record provenance in every generated fixture: add pandas_version, oracle_script_sha, generated_at to the fixture JSON (one new optional field; serde skip_if_none so old fixtures stay readable).
3. Stale-fixture detector: new scripts/check_fixture_freshness.sh compares fixture pandas_version field to oracle/requirements.txt pin. Fails CI on mismatch.
4. Quarterly regeneration ritual: document in AGENTS.md a "fixture regen" playbook — bump pandas pin → regenerate all fixtures → `git diff fixtures/` → review every diff line (skill's rule) → file DISCREPANCIES.md entries for any intentional divergences → commit atomically. First regen after this bead lands.
5. Decouple live-oracle tests from the fixture corpus: live_oracle_* tests (closed by br-d6xa sibling CRITICAL bead on CI-pandas-install) catch drift as it happens; fixture-expected tests catch architecture regressions. Both layers needed.

Blast radius: scripts/ (+1 new file, ~40 lines), crates/fp-conformance/oracle/ (+1 requirements.txt), AGENTS.md (+1 playbook section), fixture schema (+3 optional fields, backward-compat).

Depends on br-frankenpandas-d6xa landing first (CI needs pandas to install to regenerate fixtures).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:11:20.990363375Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:10:30.408679761Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:10:30.406780647Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","drift","provenance","review"]}
